hypothesis: Incorporating temporal degradation dynamics into foundation model training by pre-training on time-series electrolyte cycling data alongside SMILES representations will enable FM-based predictors to directly forecast capacity fade trajectories rather than single-point device performance metrics, with the chemical similarity-based OOD error estimation framework demonstrating a stronger inverse correlation (steeper slope m in MAE = m·Similarity + c) for cycle-life predictions than for static capacity measurements due to the compounding sensitivity of degradation pathways to subtle chemical moiety differences that accumulate over repeated charge-discharge cycles.
